data.title: Citi's Wealth unit launches AI assistant in partnership with Google Cloud, DeepMind
data.description:
Citigroup (C) on Wednesday said its Wealth unit has launched an AI assistant that was built using technology from Google Cloud and Google DeepMind.

The new capability will be made available to Citigold clients from this summer in a phased roll-out.

Citi Sky is expected to create a more intuitive, responsive, and personalized wealth experience for the Wealth unit clients and advisors.

"For decades, managing your financial life meant navigating apps, calls, and meetings. With Citi Sky, you simply ask – and act. This is the shift from interface to intelligence, from transactions to outcomes," said Andy Sieg, head of Wealth.

"It doesn't replace our advisors – it makes them more powerful, extending their reach and deepening their impact. In fact, Citi Wealth plans to add advisors in the years ahead," said Sieg.

"With Gemini Enterprise as the backbone of Citi Sky, combined with frontier models from Google DeepMind, Citi Wealth is establishing a new blueprint for how agentic AI can drive high-quality, personalized financial insights for millions of its customers," said Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian.

data.title: Honeywell says Quantinuum unit confidentially filed IPO
data.description:
• Honeywell (HON) said on Wednesday that its Quantinuum quantum computing unit confidentially filed for an initial public offering on Feb. 17.
  • The number of shares to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined, Honeywell added.
  • Honeywell shares rose 1.7% in premarket trading, while other quantum computing stocks, such as IonQ (IONQ), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Quantum Computing (QUBT), and Arqit Quantum (ARQQ), were mixed.
  • Based in Charlotte, N.C., Honeywell said in January that it intended to submit an S-1 filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission for its quantum computing unit, Quantinuum, and have it go public.
  • Quantinuum raised $600M in 2025 at a $10B valuation. The unit previously raised $300M in January 2024 in a round led by JPMorgan Chase, which valued it at $5B before the deal.

data.title: Rheinmetall wins billion-euro Bundeswehr drone contract
data.description:
Germany’s Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) secured a major contract from the Bundeswehr to supply large volumes of FV-014 loitering munition systems, reinforcing investor expectations for sustained growth in Europe’s fast-expanding defense market.

The framework agreement is valued in the billion-euro range, with an initial order worth about €300 million gross, according to an announcement Wednesday. Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) said the contract will be booked in April 2026, providing a near-term boost to its already strong order backlog.

Deliveries are scheduled to begin in the first half of 2027, while qualification of the system is expected to start in the second quarter of 2026. The agreement also includes an option for a five-digit number of additional autonomous reconnaissance and strike drones.

For investors, the announcement highlights Rheinmetall’s (RNMBF) (RNMBY) growing position in one of defense’s hottest segments: low-cost autonomous weapons systems shaped by lessons from modern warfare in Ukraine and elsewhere. Demand for loitering munitions has surged as militaries seek precision strike capability without relying solely on traditional missiles or manned aircraft.

Chief Executive Armin Papperger said the company had developed the autonomous drone in a short period of time and would soon ramp production to industrial scale with high output volumes.

The FV-014 has a range of up to 100 kilometers, carries a warhead of roughly 4 kilograms and can remain airborne for as long as 70 minutes while observing targets before striking. Rheinmetall said development and production will be carried out entirely within the European Union.

That EU-based supply chain could appeal to European governments seeking strategic autonomy and faster procurement cycles as geopolitical tensions drive higher defense spending across NATO.

Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) has emerged as one of the clearest corporate beneficiaries of Europe’s rearmament push, and this latest order suggests that unmanned systems may become an increasingly meaningful contributor to revenue growth beyond its traditional ammunition, vehicles and artillery businesses.

data.title: Aclarion announces $2.5M stock repurchase plan
data.description:
• Aclarion (ACON) on Wednesday announced a share repurchase program of up to $2.5 million of the company’s outstanding common stock.
  • Aclarion expects to execute the repurchase program over the next 12 months. 
  • The company intends to fund the repurchase program using existing cash and cash equivalents.
  • As of March 31, 2026, the company had cash and cash equivalents of approximately $19.0 million.
  • ACON +4.81% premarket to $3.7.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Inhibrx cancer drug reportedly drawing interest of Merck and rivals
data.description:
Global drugmakers, including Merck (MRK), Merck KGaA (MKGAF), and Ono Pharmaceutical (OPHLF) of Japan, are seeking agreements with Inhibrx Biosciences (INBX) for a cancer medication that could be valued at more than $8B, Reuters reported on Wednesday.

According to people familiar with the matter, Inhibrx (INBX) is eyeing a joint spinoff for the antibody therapy, identified as INBRX-106, and a second experimental cancer therapy in a transaction that could potentially be worth over $9B, subject to positive clinical trial results.

The discussions for a deal are at preliminary stages, and it could be several months before the companies could reach a final agreement, the people said, seeking anonymity as the matter remains private.

INBRX-106 is currently undergoing studies as a monotherapy and in combination with Keytruda, Merck's (MRK) blockbuster cancer medication. Inhibrx (INBX) has said it believes the drug can boost the effects of MRK's anti-PD1 therapy. 

Merck (MRK), Germany’s Merck (MKKGY), and Ono (OPHLF) didn’t immediately return Reuters’ requests for comments, while Inhibrx (INBX) declined to comment.

"We think INBRX-106 is ​highly investable through targeting the narrow Keytruda-responder ​base to enhance this $32 billion ⁠drug’s cure-like efficacy," Stifel analyst Dara Azar wrote earlier this month as he initiated coverage on Inhibrx (INBX) with a Buy rating and $150 per share target.

data.title: Salesforce and Google Cloud expand AI partnership for autonomous workflows
data.description:
• Salesforce (CRM) and Google Cloud are deepening integration to enable AI agents to execute end-to-end workflows across both platforms.
  • The partnership aims to solve fragmented data and disconnected systems while reducing data movement and context switching.
  • AI agents will operate within tools like Slack and Google Workspace.
  • Platforms including Agentforce and Gemini Enterprise will provide intelligence and contextual decision-making.
  • The collaboration supports a shift from manual processes to more autonomous, AI-driven enterprise operations.
  • Integrations will span multiple layers of enterprise systems, including context, workflow execution, and user engagement.
  • CRM shares up 1% premarket.

data.title: McKesson declares $0.82 dividend
data.description:
• McKesson (MCK) declares $0.82/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.39%
  • Payable July 1; for shareholders of record June 1; ex-div June 1.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.82 for four consecutive quarters.
  • See MCK Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: RenX sees Q1 revenue above $3.5M, signaling sequential growth
data.description:
RenX Enterprises (RENX) Wednesday said it expects to report revenue in excess of $3.5M for the first quarter of 2026, reflecting sequential growth over the fourth quarter of 2025.

The expected growth reflects anticipated stronger material sales at the company’s Myakka City organics processing facility and an expected meaningful step-up in logistics revenue across the company’s transportation operations.

First-quarter material sales at Myakka City are expected to include processed mulch, compost, and engineered soil products, reflecting the sequential growth from the company's fourth quarter of 2025. This growth is supported by strong customer demand for the company’s highly processed mulch and engineered soil product lines. 

The company’s logistics segment, operated through its Zimmer Equipment operations, is also expected to report higher revenue for the first quarter of 2026 relative to the fourth quarter of 2025, supported by strong route utilization and continued hauling activity across its organic waste transportation network.
